notes: Crashes with "InitRender" popup error on startup.
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes: Now works with Proton 4.11-1, did not try multiplayer yet.
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes: Offline was playable with a consistent 60fps. "Performance mode" option crashes the game. Attempting to play online does not work and crashes the application immediately, which took 4 seconds to verify and is critical to test before rating a fighting game.
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes: Offline play works fine. Going into online mode instantly crashes most of the time, but occasionally it works and that session will then work online mostly without issues. Played a total of about 50 games with a friend, with 2 crashes to desktop, not counting the numerous crashes from trying to access online mode in the first place.
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes: Boots, plays all game sounds, but displays a black screen.
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes: It works since arrival of proton 4.11-1. Online matches work but it often crashes in the menus. Netcode seems to be similar quality. Offline game modes renders and plays well. Just no video playback for opening and ending. (To fix black screen issue, edit bbconfigs.ini file and append ""iDisplayMode=2" to launch in fullscreen. Later go in options and turn off anti-aliasing for window modes.)
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes: Initially only got a black screen. Edited bbconfigs.ini file and added ""iDisplayMode=2", which made the game launch in fullscreen mode. Game now works flawlessly, at least in offline play. Couldn't test online since I couldn't find any matches.
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes:
Blackscreen if you don't change bbconfig.ini:

 iDisplayMode=2
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle

notes:
It runs great after making the configuration that almost everybody commented before, the file ''bbconfigs.ini'' is in ''Save'' folder.
Its a shame that the online doesn't works but welp, at least now is playable
title: BlazBlue: Cross Tag Battle
